# Copilot instructions — ha-MU-TH-UR-6000-cards

> Canonical standards live in the `dev-standards` repo on SOUNDWAVE/Gitea.
> Read by Copilot chat **and** inline suggestions.

## What this repo is

A **Home Assistant Lovelace card pack** (HACS frontend/plugin) — a suite of
Alien "MU-TH-UR 6000"–themed cards (alarm, button, clock, gauge, glance, light,
picture, sensor, status, text, weather). **JavaScript**, rollup-built from `src/`
into a single `dist/` bundle. Not a `custom_components/` integration.

## Repo shape

- `src/*.js` — one file per card + `base-card.js` + `mu-th-ur-6000-cards.js`
  (entry) + `fonts.css`.
- `dist/mu-th-ur-6000-cards.js` (+ `.map`) — rollup **build output** (the bundle
  HACS serves).
- `fonts/` — bundled **Thedus** `.otf` fonts (licensed — there's a commercial-use
  license PDF under `mother-examples/Thedus-FONT/`). Don't remove/replace these.
- `rollup.config.js`, `package.json` (+ lock), `.eslintrc.json`, `hacs.json`,
  `info.md`, `examples/`, `test.html`.
- `.github/workflows/` — `build.yml` + `validate.yaml` (HACS validation).

## Conventions

- HACS **plugin** (frontend): **no `manifest.json`/`hassfest`/pytest**.
- **Edit `src/*.js`, then rebuild — never hand-edit `dist/`** (generated by rollup).
- npm deps; commit `package-lock.json`. CI builds on push.
- Bump `version` in `package.json` + `hacs.json` for releases.
- The bundled fonts are licensed assets — keep the license PDF with them.

## Never

- Don't hand-edit `dist/`. Don't strip the licensed fonts. Don't commit secrets.
